appeal_letter:
Dear UnitedHealthcare Appeals Review,

I am writing to appeal the denial of transfer to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (IRF) for [Member Name], dated [date]. The denial relies on a physical therapy note documenting fatigue after 30 minutes of therapy. This appeal asks you to review the full clinical record, which shows that note reflects a single session on the day of stroke onset — not the patient's current functional capacity.

The patient sustained a severe stroke and began rehabilitation immediately. The note cited in the denial was recorded during the patient's first therapy session, the day immediately following the stroke event. Since that session, the patient has consistently completed two hours of intensive therapy per day without significant fatigue, as documented in the daily therapy logs attached. The UHC threshold of three hours of intensive therapy per day is a standard the patient is already approaching in the acute hospital setting.

Placing this patient in a Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) rather than an IRF carries a direct clinical consequence: SNFs provide a lower intensity of rehabilitation that has been shown to result in significantly worse functional outcomes for post-stroke patients with active recovery potential. This patient's trajectory supports IRF-level care.

We ask that the reviewer consider the updated daily therapy logs and the therapist's current assessment, rather than the initial session note, in determining eligibility.

We request approval of IRF transfer. Please confirm receipt and the deadline for any further submission.

This letter is a draft for your review.

feature_scores.evidence: Directly addresses the specific note cited in the denial and explains its temporal context.
Correctly identifies the vector: stale clinical notes misrepresenting current patient capacity.
Names the SNF vs IRF outcome differential as a concrete clinical risk of the denial standing.

outcome.critique: Well-targeted rebuttal that identifies the specific note used in the denial and corrects the factual record with updated documentation.
rationale: The denial rests on a single outlier data point that the updated therapy logs directly contradict.
SNF vs IRF outcome framing adds concrete clinical stakes to the appeal.

risk_flags: Outcome depends on whether the updated therapy logs are accepted as timely new information.

mirror.what_was_denied: Prior authorization for transfer to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (IRF) following a stroke.
mirror.why_they_said_no: They cite a physical therapy note showing the patient fatigued after 30 minutes of therapy, falling short of the 3-hour daily threshold for IRF admission.
mirror.deadline_note: Check your denial letter for the appeal filing deadline and submit before that date.
mirror.strongest_angle: The note UHC relied on was from the very first therapy session, the day of the stroke — updated logs show the patient has been completing two hours a day since then.

citations_used.title: Denial letter
citations_used.quote: clinical notes from your physical therapist in the hospital indicate you fatigued after 30 minutes of therapy yesterday

missing_evidence_checklist: Daily physical therapy session logs from the days following the initial session
Current therapist's written assessment of the patient's rehabilitation potential
Physician's letter explaining why SNF-level care is clinically insufficient for this presentation
